title: DemonKenny Loot Ores Mod
description: a forge mod for Minecraft 1.7.10 that adds some new ores and items, as well as an item that can self renew with it's drops
body:
This is a mod I created using a public tutorial. Due to copyright law, and the modrinth rules, all assets in this mod were recreated to be legally my own original work, that was the only issue that might have prevented me from uploading this in it's old form, apologies to the modrinth moderation team for that one. Wish I'd considered that before my first upload attempt under another project name.

 
WHAT THIS MOD ADDS TO THE GAME


Adds an item called Blue Glowstone (the redesign makes it look a bit odd, sorry about that), it is craftable from the drop obtained from Special Ore


 I added several crafting and smelting recipes to the items in this mod. Some of the items have multiple recipes.


 The mod tools are now obtainable by smelting specific vanilla tools.


 I added the custom ores in this mod to the world generation table. They spawn in all 3 of the vanilla worlds (overworld, nether, 
 end)


I added a way for the special multi ore to be crafted  based on it's item drops.


 I created a specific set of recipies allowing infinite creation of specific items from a specific ore as the item to create the ore is also dropped from itself, drops include diamonds, emeralds, gold, iron, coal, redstone, and lapis.  Lapis and restone can be crafted together to make the ore, emeralds can be smelted into etiher the item to make the ore, or the ore itself, I forget which one as I made this mod years ago, and if memory serves me right, the ore might also drop the item used to make it directly.

  


This mod appears as "It's A Secret!" in the mods list in game, as that was the original name of the mod, however, due to a rule on CurseForge, the site I had posted it initially, I had to rename the project, so I apologize for any confusion. When I get around to updating this mod again, I'll rename it. The versions also have conflicting info, it lists as v2.1.0 in one spot, and v1.0.0 in another, need to find out why.
